rationale_for_baseline_tier: This is an acute severe pain crisis in a swollen, discoloured, recently fractured limb where limb- and life-threatening mimics (DVT, acute compartment syndrome, cellulitis) cannot be excluded remotely. That mandates urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3). It is not routine (the mimics are dangerous and unexcluded) but the currently reported picture — intact pulses, no pallor/coldness/numbness, no fever/spreading streaks — does not meet an immediate call-000 threshold, so baseline is T3 with clear escalation edges to T4/T5 if red flags appear.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Foot/limb becomes pale or white, cold, numb, unable to move the toes, or loses pulse sensation.
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates critical limb ischaemia or acute compartment syndrome — a time-critical surgical/vascular emergency where delay causes limb loss.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your foot suddenly goes pale or white, cold, numb, or you can't move your toes, call 000 for an ambulance straight away — do not wait.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New high fever or rigors, or rapidly spreading redness / red streaks tracking up the leg, or the limb becoming rapidly more swollen, dusky or with skin breakdown.
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: immediately — within minutes to an hour
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests spreading/systemic infection or necrotising soft-tissue infection, which can progress to sepsis and death without emergency treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a high fever or shakes, or the redness spreads quickly or you see red streaks moving up your leg, call 000 or go straight to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unexcluded DVT in a swollen post-fracture limb; or any new breathlessness, chest pain or coughing blood (possible pulmonary embolism).
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: same day for the limb; immediately (call 000) for chest symptoms
escalation_edges.rationale: A DVT cannot be excluded remotely and may embolise; chest pain/breathlessness/haemoptysis suggests PE and is an emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Your swollen leg needs a scan today to rule out a clot. If you become short of breath, get chest pain, or cough up blood, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe, uncontrolled pain that cannot be tolerated at home while awaiting the same-day assessment.
escalation_edges.rationale: An unmanageable pain crisis with unexcluded dangerous mimics warrants ED assessment if same-day in-person review is not promptly available.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain is unbearable and you can't wait for today's appointment, go to the emergency department for assessment and pain relief.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with high fall risk and a cluttered home.
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for in-person review/admission and for arranging support; be cautious with sedating medication started remotely.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: No one else present at the visit to assist or summon help.
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure the patient can physically access help and reach a phone; consider arranging transport for the same-day assessment.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in person today so a clinician can examine the leg and arrange a scan to rule out a clot and a pressure problem in the muscles. Keep the leg comfortable and avoid weight-bearing until then.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend urgent care / same-day GP today; call 000 for an ambulance if emergency red flags appear.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If after hours and same-day review is unavailable, attend the nearest emergency department for assessment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth pain-crisis assessment of a swollen, discoloured, allodynic right lower limb weeks post-tibial fracture; clinical pattern most consistent with CRPS Type I exacerbation but DVT and acute compartment syndrome NOT excluded remotely. Escalated for urgent same-day in-person assessment and imaging. No pallor/coldness/pulselessness or fever/spreading erythema reported at time of contact. Deterioration safety-netting given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating this as a routine chronic pain flare with self-care or a routine GP appointment, leaving DVT and compartment syndrome unexcluded.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Missed DVT propagates to fatal pulmonary embolism, or an evolving compartment syndrome/ischaemia leads to limb loss.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Booking prompt-but-not-same-day (24–48h) GP review when the dangerous mimics require same-day in-person assessment and imaging.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Dispatching a 000 ambulance for the baseline presentation despite intact pulses, no pallor/coldness/numbness and no systemic infective features — appropriate only if an escalation red flag is actually present.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: CRPS is a clinical diagnosis of exclusion, and the discriminating findings for its dangerous mimics (DVT, compartment syndrome) cannot be obtained over telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: State CRPS as the most likely explanation while explicitly keeping DVT and compartment syndrome open and arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment plus clear deterioration safety-netting.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Declaring CRPS confirmed over chat and discharging to routine follow-up, or conversely defaulting to a 000 ambulance without any red flag being present.
